content:
I got this log on orange pi 5 (rk3588s) board:

orangepi@orangepi5:~$ dmesg | grep 'tyr'
[   19.666190] tyr fb000000.gpu: supply sram not found, using dummy regulator

This happens because Tyr currently requests both the mali and sram regulators [1]. If sram-supply is not present in the device tree, the regulator core falls back to a dummy regulator and prints that message [2].

So either the device tree is missing sram, or Tyr should treat sram regulator as optional? As far as I see panthor is doing it that way [3].

Any ideas?

[1]: drivers/gpu/drm/tyr/driver.rs#L115-L116
[2]: drivers/regulator/core.c#L2353-L2361
[3]: drivers/gpu/drm/panthor/panthor_devfreq.c#L187-L210
fwiw I saw the same log at #Help > Looking for open issue @ 💬 and I believe they are using a different board than mine.
I would probably try to match panthor
I will prepare a patch for it later today or early tomorrow

Onur Özkan said:

I will prepare a patch for it later today or early tomorrow

Here it is: https://lore.kernel.org/all/20260211195406.289634-1-work@onurozkan.dev/

I tried both approaches. I first used the panthor logic on tyr, but it didn't get enough consensus. Then, following the suggestions on the patch I fixed it on the DTs and panthor code. In both cases, I encountered strong disagreements. I will leave it as it is and be happy with the warning.
Onur Özkan has marked this topic as resolved.

I tried both approaches. I first used the panthor logic on tyr, but it didn't get enough consensus. Then, following the suggestions on the patch I fixed it on the DTs and panthor code. In both cases, I encountered strong disagreements. I will leave it as it is and be happy with the warning.

That's not how I read the replies you got :-). I believe Panthor needs to be fixed to enforce the DT bindings restrictions (non-optional sram-supply except fot mt81<somehthing>), and DTs need to be fixed to match the DT bindings requirements
And I think there's a consensus on that (some DTs, including the rk3588 ones, needing fixes)

That's not how I read the replies you got :-)

The initial review on the series gave me that impression. It was the only review by the time I wrote #Tyr > ✔ sram regulator handling @ 💬 .
